articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutionality of Uniform Civil Code Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A nine-judge bench of the Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, upheld the constitutional validity of the Union Government's framework for implementing a Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
• The Court affirmed Parliament's legislative competence to enact a UCC, stating it aligns with Article 44 of the Directive Principles of State Policy.
• While acknowledging concerns about diversity, the judgment emphasized that a UCC aims for gender justice and equality, harmonizing personal laws without eroding essential religious practices.
• The ruling provides a significant legal impetus for the government to proceed with a phased implementation strategy, potentially starting with specific aspects of personal law.
• Dissenting opinions focused on the potential impact on minority rights and the need for broader societal consensus before such a sweeping reform.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Article 44 mandates the state to endeavor to secure a UCC for citizens.
UCC is listed under Part IV of the Indian Constitution (DPSP).
The Supreme Court has previously called for the implementation of UCC in several cases.
The judgment was delivered by a nine-judge bench, indicating a matter of significant constitutional importance.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on the future of personal laws in India and its alignment with the principles of gender justice and equality.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in implementing a Uniform Civil Code, considering India's diverse socio-religious fabric and the federal structure.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in interpreting constitutional provisions like Article 44 and shaping social reforms in the country.
Examine the arguments for and against the UCC, including concerns about minority rights versus the pursuit of national integration.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of the Supreme Court of India building with legal documents and scales of justice.
articles.trivia: Goa is currently the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code, inherited from its Portuguese colonial past.

articles.headline: Manipur MLA Vungzagin Valte Passes Away at 61
articles.explainer:
• Vungzagin Valte, a sitting Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Manipur, passed away at the age of 61.
• He represented the Thanlon Assembly constituency in Pherzawl district, having been elected multiple times since 2012.
• Valte was a prominent political figure in the Kuki-Zo community and had served in various capacities, including as a cabinet minister.
• His demise creates a vacancy in the Manipur Legislative Assembly, necessitating a by-election within six months as per constitutional provisions.
• The political landscape of Manipur, particularly in the hill districts, is likely to see shifts following the loss of a seasoned leader.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: An MLA is a representative elected by the voters of an electoral district (constituency) to the legislature of a state government in the Indian system.
A vacancy in the state legislative assembly requires a by-election within six months.
Manipur is a state in Northeast India.
The strength of a state legislative assembly varies based on population and is determined by constitutional provisions (Article 170).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of regional political leadership in maintaining stability and representing diverse communities in states like Manipur.
Analyze the constitutional provisions and electoral process related to filling vacancies in state legislative assemblies through by-elections.
Examine the challenges faced by electoral democracy in India's North-Eastern states, including issues of representation and political participation.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait photo of Vungzagin Valte, former Manipur MLA.
articles.trivia: The minimum age to be an MLA in India is 25 years.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Global Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive policy review.
• This decision comes amidst persistent global inflationary pressures and geopolitical uncertainties, balancing growth objectives with inflation control.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted concerns over food inflation and the need to ensure inflation aligns with the 4% target on a durable basis.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation remains within the target while supporting growth.
• Market analysts anticipate a 'higher for longer' interest rate regime, with potential rate cuts pushed further into the year.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: The Repo Rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks in the event of any shortfall of funds.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
RBI's inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst current domestic and global economic conditions.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth, particularly in the context of supply-side pressures and global spillovers.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing persistent inflation and supporting sustainable economic recovery in India.
Examine the implications of a 'higher for longer' interest rate regime on various sectors of the Indian economy, including investment, consumption, and credit growth.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade with monetary policy charts in the background.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established in 1935 under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Focus on Indo-Pacific Security and Green Economy
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming their commitment to a Free and Open Indo-Pacific (FOIP).
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, maritime security, and intelligence sharing, particularly concerning regional challenges.
• Both nations pledged to deepen collaboration in emerging technologies, including quantum computing, AI, and semiconductors, to build resilient supply chains.
• A significant outcome was the agreement to fast-track joint projects in green energy, hydrogen production, and electric vehicle infrastructure.
• The dialogue also emphasized strengthening multilateral cooperation through platforms like the Quad and the G20, addressing global issues such as climate change and sustainable development.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) alongside the USA and Australia.
Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
Both countries participate in joint military exercises such as Dharma Guardian (Army) and JIMEX (Navy).
The concept of a 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' is a cornerstone of their shared vision.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving strategic convergence between India and Japan in the Indo-Pacific region, citing key areas of cooperation and shared interests.
Discuss the significance of the India-Japan partnership in promoting regional stability and economic prosperity, particularly in the context of geopolitical shifts.
Evaluate the potential for enhanced collaboration in critical and emerging technologies between India and Japan, and its implications for national security and economic resilience.
Examine how the bilateral relationship contributes to India's 'Act East Policy' and Japan's broader foreign policy objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags displayed during a joint press conference.
articles.trivia: Japan is India's largest ODA (Official Development Assistance) partner.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype 'Pushpak-2'
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful experimental flight test of its advanced Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype, codenamed 'Pushpak-2'.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment simulating a re-entry trajectory, demonstrating autonomous navigation, guidance, and control systems.
• This marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) launch vehicle, aiming to drastically reduce space launch costs.
• The 'Pushpak-2' incorporates advanced heat shield materials and aerodynamic designs optimized for atmospheric re-entry and hypersonic flight.
• ISRO aims to achieve operational reusable launch capabilities within the next decade, bolstering India's competitive edge in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) project began with the winged body 'HEX' mission.
Reusable launch vehicles aim to reduce the cost of space access by avoiding the need to build new rockets for each launch.
Key challenges in RLV development include heat management during re-entry and precise landing mechanisms.
Global players like SpaceX (Falcon 9) and Blue Origin are also pursuing reusable rocket technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its potential impact on commercial space exploration.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in designing and testing RLVs, focusing on aspects like materials science, aerodynamics, and propulsion systems.
Evaluate how 'Pushpak-2' and future RLV advancements can enhance India's self-reliance in space and contribute to its economic development.
Compare India's RLV efforts with global trends in reusable space technology and assess its competitive position.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's 'Pushpak-2' RLV prototype during atmospheric re-entry.
articles.trivia: The name 'Pushpak' is derived from the mythical flying chariot Pushpaka Vimana from the ancient Indian epic Ramayana.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'National Urban Air Quality Improvement Mission' to Tackle City Pollution
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched the 'National Urban Air Quality Improvement Mission' (NUQIM), a comprehensive initiative targeting air pollution in 150 non-attainment cities.
• The mission adopts a multi-pronged strategy, including stricter emission norms for industries, promotion of electric vehicles, enhanced public transport, and green urban planning.
• It introduces a performance-linked funding mechanism for states and urban local bodies based on their progress in reducing PM2.5 and PM10 levels.
• NUQIM also emphasizes public awareness campaigns, capacity building for local authorities, and the deployment of advanced air quality monitoring networks.
• This initiative is an extension of previous efforts like the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) but with a stronger focus on integrated urban planning and citizen participation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
keyTerms: National Urban Air Quality Improvement Mission (NUQIM)
prelimsPoints: PM2.5 and PM10 are fine particulate matter linked to respiratory and cardiovascular diseases.
Non-attainment cities are those that consistently fail to meet national ambient air quality standards.
The National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) was launched in 2019 to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024 (from 2017 levels).
Air Quality Index (AQI) is a tool used to communicate air quality status to people.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the 'National Urban Air Quality Improvement Mission' (NUQIM) in comparison to previous initiatives like NCAP, highlighting its strengths and potential weaknesses.
Discuss the multi-sectoral approach required to tackle urban air pollution in India, considering its various sources and socio-economic dimensions.
Examine the role of technology, policy, and public participation in achieving sustainable air quality improvements in Indian cities.
Analyze the challenges in implementing such missions across diverse urban landscapes and the importance of inter-state and inter-agency coordination.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing a clean city skyline with green trees and minimal smog, representing improved air quality.
articles.trivia: According to WHO, air pollution is the largest environmental health risk globally.

articles.headline: Border Roads Organisation Inaugurates Strategic Tunnel in Arunachal Pradesh, Boosting Border Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) inaugurated the 'Sela-Tawang Tunnel Bypass', a crucial strategic tunnel connecting Bomdila with Tawang in Arunachal Pradesh.
• The 2.5 km twin-tube tunnel, along with an approach road, provides all-weather connectivity, significantly reducing travel time and improving military logistics to the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• Constructed at an altitude of over 13,000 feet, the tunnel bypasses the challenging Sela Pass, which often remains snow-bound for several months a year.
• This infrastructure project is part of India's broader strategy to enhance connectivity and develop border infrastructure in the Eastern Himalayas.
• The tunnel will not only aid defence forces but also boost tourism and socio-economic development in the remote Tawang region.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is a road construction executive force in India that develops and maintains road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.
Sela Pass is a high-altitude mountain pass in Arunachal Pradesh.
Tawang is a strategically important town in Arunachal Pradesh, close to the LAC.
The tunnel project is part of the larger Sela Tunnel project which includes two tunnels and an approach road.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of border infrastructure development, such as the Sela-Tawang Tunnel Bypass, for India's national security and military preparedness along the LAC.
Analyze the role of the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) in enhancing connectivity in challenging terrains and its contribution to India's defence capabilities.
Examine the socio-economic impact of improved connectivity in remote border regions like Tawang, including its potential for tourism and local development.
Evaluate the challenges involved in constructing high-altitude infrastructure projects and the innovative engineering solutions employed by agencies like BRO.
articles.imageDescription: Entrance of the newly inaugurated Sela-Tawang Tunnel in a mountainous region.
articles.trivia: BRO was formed on 7 May 1960 and operates under the Ministry of Defence.

articles.headline: Ministry of Health Unveils New National Mental Health Action Plan 2026-2030
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched the 'National Mental Health Action Plan 2026-2030', a five-year roadmap to strengthen mental healthcare services across India.
• The plan focuses on integrating mental health into primary healthcare, increasing the availability of trained professionals, and leveraging digital health solutions.
• Key objectives include universal screening for common mental disorders, reducing the treatment gap, and combating stigma associated with mental illness.
• It allocates increased funding for mental health infrastructure, including district mental health programmes and tele-manas services.
• The action plan emphasizes a community-based approach, promoting mental well-being and early intervention, with a special focus on vulnerable populations like adolescents and the elderly.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: The Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, decriminalized suicide and provides for rights of persons with mental illness.
National Mental Health Programme (NMHP) was launched in 1982.
Tele-MANAS (Tele-Mental Health Assistance and Networking Across States) is a 24/7 mental health helpline.
India faces a significant mental health treatment gap.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the 'National Mental Health Action Plan 2026-2030', highlighting its strengths in addressing India's mental health crisis and potential challenges in implementation.
Discuss the importance of integrating mental healthcare into primary health services and the role of digital platforms like Tele-MANAS in expanding access.
Examine the socio-economic factors contributing to mental health issues in India and the societal challenges in combating stigma and promoting mental well-being.
Suggest further policy measures and community-level interventions that could enhance the effectiveness of mental health initiatives in India.
articles.imageDescription: Symbolic image of a brain with intertwined hands, representing mental health support and care.
articles.trivia: Globally, one in eight people live with a mental disorder.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Gains Traction at G20 Finance Ministers Meet
articles.explainer:
• India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, exemplified by Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, received significant praise and interest at the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting.
• Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman presented the success story of India's DPI in driving financial inclusion, digital payments, and efficient public service delivery.
• Several G20 nations and international organizations expressed keen interest in adopting or adapting India's open-source, interoperable DPI frameworks for their own digital transformation.
• Discussions focused on cross-border payments, data governance, and building inclusive digital economies using lessons from the Indian model.
• India reaffirmed its commitment to sharing its DPI expertise with developing nations, promoting a 'global good' approach to digital transformation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number issued by the UIDAI.
UPI is an instant real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
DigiLocker is a cloud-based platform for issuance and verification of documents.
India's DPI stack is often referred to as the 'India Stack'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model in fostering financial inclusion, governance, and economic growth within India and for other developing nations.
Discuss the key components of the India Stack (e.g., Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker) and how their interoperability drives efficiency and innovation in public service delivery.
Evaluate the opportunities and challenges for other G20 nations in adopting or adapting India's DPI model, considering issues of data privacy, regulatory frameworks, and technological scalability.
Examine India's role in promoting a global digital public good and its implications for international cooperation in digital transformation.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating interconnected digital services like UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker, representing India's DPI.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processes more transactions than credit cards and debit cards combined in India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Inclusive growth and issues arising from it

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms in Anti-Defection Law
articles.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee submitted its report recommending significant amendments to the Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution, commonly known as the Anti-Defection Law.
• The committee proposed restricting the Speaker's discretionary powers in deciding defection cases and suggested a fixed timeframe for such decisions, possibly within three months.
• It also recommended clarifying the definition of 'voluntary giving up of membership' and 'merger' to prevent misuse and ensure legislative stability.
• The report advocated for the Election Commission's role in advising on defection cases to ensure impartiality and reduce political influence.
• These reforms aim to curb frequent political defections, strengthen party discipline, and uphold the spirit of democratic mandates.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: The Tenth Schedule was added to the Indian Constitution by the 52nd Amendment Act of 1985.
The law applies to both Parliament and State Legislatures.
The Speaker (or Chairman in Rajya Sabha) is the final authority in deciding defection cases.
The Supreme Court in Kihoto Hollohan v. Zachillhu (1992) upheld the constitutional validity of the Tenth Schedule but subjected the Speaker's decision to judicial review.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the effectiveness of the Anti-Defection Law in curbing political defections and maintaining legislative stability in India since its enactment.
Discuss the challenges arising from the Speaker's discretionary powers in deciding defection cases and how the proposed reforms aim to address these issues.
Analyze the implications of involving the Election Commission in advising on defection cases for ensuring impartiality and strengthening democratic processes.
Suggest other potential reforms or constitutional amendments that could further enhance the efficacy of the Anti-Defection Law and prevent its misuse.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building with a gavel and legal documents, symbolizing legislative reforms.
articles.trivia: The 91st Amendment Act of 2003 removed the provision that allowed a split in a political party (one-third of members) to be exempt from defection.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework for Green Hydrogen Production and Export
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government launched a comprehensive 'National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0' policy framework, aimed at accelerating domestic production and positioning India as a global hub for green hydrogen.
• The policy introduces enhanced incentives for green hydrogen and green ammonia production, including capital subsidies and production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolyzer manufacturing.
• It mandates blending of green hydrogen in specific industrial sectors (e.g., refineries, fertilizer production) and promotes its use in transport and energy storage.
• The framework also outlines strategies for developing a robust green hydrogen export ecosystem, including infrastructure for storage and transportation.
• This initiative is critical for India's energy transition, decarbonization goals, and achieving net-zero emissions by 2070.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India launched its initial National Green Hydrogen Mission in January 2023.
Green ammonia is produced using green hydrogen and nitrogen, with renewable energy sources.
Electrolyzers are devices that split water into hydrogen and oxygen.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the 'National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0' policy framework in accelerating India's energy transition and achieving its climate targets.
Discuss the economic and technological challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production in India and the role of government incentives in overcoming them.
Evaluate India's potential to become a global hub for green hydrogen export, considering its renewable energy resources and strategic location.
Examine the cross-sectoral applications of green hydrogen and its role in decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and transportation.
articles.imageDescription: Stylized image showing green hydrogen flame and renewable energy sources like wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the Universe.

articles.headline: India Pledges Strong Support for African Union's Permanent Seat in UNSC
articles.explainer:
• During a high-level visit to the African Union (AU) headquarters, India's External Affairs Minister reiterated India's strong and unequivocal support for a permanent seat for the African Union in a reformed United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
• This pledge aligns with India's consistent call for comprehensive UNSC reforms to make it more representative, legitimate, and effective.
• India emphasized the need for greater representation of developing countries, particularly from Africa, which has historically been underrepresented in global decision-making bodies.
• The move is seen as a significant diplomatic gesture to strengthen India-Africa relations and foster South-South cooperation.
• Both sides stressed the importance of multilateralism and collective efforts to address global challenges, including climate change, terrorism, and sustainable development.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The UNSC has 15 members: 5 permanent (P5) and 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.
The P5 members are China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States.
India is a member of the G4 group (along with Brazil, Germany, and Japan) that advocates for permanent seats for themselves and Africa in the UNSC.
The African Union consists of 55 member states located on the continent of Africa.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind India's consistent support for the African Union's permanent seat in a reformed UNSC and its implications for India's foreign policy objectives.
Discuss the arguments for and against the expansion of the UNSC's permanent membership, considering issues of effectiveness, representation, and geopolitical balance.
Examine the broader significance of India-Africa cooperation in shaping a more equitable and representative global order, particularly through multilateral institutions.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving comprehensive UN Security Council reforms and the role of various stakeholders in this process.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the African Union displayed side-by-side during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: The African Union was formed in 2002, succeeding the Organisation of African Unity (OAU).

articles.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous 6G Technology Development, India Aims for Global Leadership
articles.explainer:
• Indian researchers at IIT-Delhi and the Department of Telecommunications (DoT) announced a significant breakthrough in the development of indigenous 6G communication technology.
• The achievement involves successful lab-scale demonstration of terahertz communication modules and AI-driven network management protocols, crucial for future 6G networks.
• India's 'Bharat 6G Alliance' (B6GA) is accelerating research and development, aiming to establish India as a key contributor to global 6G standards.
• The government plans to allocate substantial funding for R&D, pilot projects, and international collaborations to fast-track commercial deployment by 2030.
• This initiative is poised to enhance India's digital sovereignty, create new economic opportunities, and bridge the digital divide in remote areas.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: 6G is the successor to 5G technology, expected to offer significantly higher speeds, lower latency, and greater capacity.
Terahertz frequencies (0.1 THz to 10 THz) are being explored for 6G for ultra-high data rates.
AI and Machine Learning are expected to play a crucial role in managing and optimizing 6G networks.
India launched the Bharat 6G Alliance (B6GA) in 2023 to foster innovation in 6G technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential transformative impact of indigenous 6G technology on various sectors of the Indian economy and society, including healthcare, education, and manufacturing.
Analyze the technological advancements and challenges associated with 6G development, particularly focusing on terahertz communication and AI integration.
Evaluate the strategic importance of India's efforts to achieve global leadership in 6G, considering aspects of digital sovereignty, economic competitiveness, and national security.
Examine the role of government, academia, and industry collaboration through initiatives like B6GA in driving technological innovation and policy formulation for future communication networks.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract digital graphic representing 6G network connectivity with speed lines and data flow.
articles.trivia: The first generation (1G) of mobile telephony was introduced in the 1980s, offering analog voice calls.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Challenges in Implementing National Education Policy 2020
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by the Ministry of Education highlighted significant challenges in the nationwide implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Key issues identified include inadequate funding for infrastructure upgrades, shortage of trained teachers, especially for foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) programs, and resistance to curriculum reforms.
• The report also pointed to disparities in implementation across states, with some lagging due to administrative hurdles and lack of decentralized planning.
• While acknowledging progress in certain areas like digital learning initiatives, the report called for greater inter-ministerial coordination and community involvement.
• Recommendations include increased budgetary allocation, focused teacher training programs, and flexible implementation strategies tailored to regional contexts.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
Its vision is to transform India's education system to meet the needs of the 21st century.
Key pillars include access, equity, quality, affordability, and accountability.
The policy proposes a new 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the major challenges identified in the implementation of the National Education Policy 2020 across different levels of education in India.
Discuss the importance of adequate funding, teacher capacity building, and decentralized planning for the successful realization of NEP's objectives.
Examine how the disparities in implementation across states can exacerbate educational inequalities and suggest strategies to foster equitable progress.
Evaluate the long-term implications of successful or unsuccessful NEP implementation on India's human resource development and economic growth.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a classroom setting with a teacher, symbolizing education and learning.
articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) recommended spending 6% of GDP on education, a target yet to be consistently met.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education

articles.headline: SEBI Proposes Stricter Norms for Social Stock Exchange Listings
articles.explainer:
• The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) released a consultation paper proposing stricter eligibility and disclosure norms for entities listing on the Social Stock Exchange (SSE).
• The proposed amendments aim to enhance transparency, accountability, and investor confidence in the SSE, ensuring that only genuine social enterprises benefit from public funding.
• Key proposals include mandating impact reporting by independent auditors, increasing minimum issue size for fundraising, and stringent criteria for defining 'social intent and impact'.
• The move comes amidst concerns about potential 'impact washing' and the need to protect investors in this nascent segment of the capital market.
• Stakeholders are invited to provide feedback on the proposed regulations, which are expected to streamline operations and strengthen the SSE ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Government Budgeting.
keyTerms: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
prelimsPoints: The Social Stock Exchange (SSE) was introduced in India in 2022 to facilitate fundraising for social enterprises.
SEBI is the regulatory body for the securities market in India.
Social enterprises can raise funds through equity, debt, or Zero Coupon Zero Principal (ZCZP) instruments on the SSE.
The concept of SSE was recommended by a working group set up by SEBI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives behind the establishment of the Social Stock Exchange (SSE) in India and its potential to channel capital towards social development.
Discuss the rationale behind SEBI's proposed stricter norms for SSE listings, considering the challenges of 'impact washing' and ensuring investor protection.
Evaluate the role of impact reporting and independent audits in fostering transparency and accountability within the social enterprise ecosystem.
Examine how a well-regulated SSE can contribute to inclusive growth and address societal challenges by providing a credible fundraising platform for non-profit and for-profit social enterprises.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic depicting a stock market ticker with 'SSE' and 'Impact' keywords, symbolizing the Social Stock Exchange.
articles.trivia: India is one of the few countries globally to have a dedicated Social Stock Exchange.

articles.headline: High Court Stays Local Body Elections in State X Due to Delimitation Irregularities
articles.explainer:
• The High Court of State X issued an interim stay on the upcoming local body elections, citing significant irregularities in the delimitation process of wards and constituencies.
• Petitions filed by various political parties and civil society groups alleged that the delimitation exercise was conducted arbitrarily, violating principles of population equality and geographical compactness.
• The court observed prima facie evidence of gerrymandering and ordered the State Election Commission (SEC) to review and rectify the delimitation process within a specified timeframe.
• This decision has led to political uproar and logistical challenges for the SEC, which had already initiated election preparations.
• The ruling underscores the judiciary's role in upholding electoral fairness and ensuring the constitutional mandate of free and fair elections at the grassroots level.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Local body elections (Panchayats and Municipalities) are conducted by the State Election Commission (SEC).
Delimitation is the act or process of fixing limits or boundaries of territorial constituencies in a country or a province having a legislative body.
Articles 243K and 243ZA of the Constitution deal with elections to Panchayats and Municipalities, respectively.
The High Court has powers of judicial review over the decisions of the SEC, especially concerning delimitation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the importance of fair and transparent delimitation processes for ensuring equitable representation in local self-governance institutions in India.
Discuss the constitutional role of the State Election Commission (SEC) in conducting local body elections and the challenges it faces, including political interference and administrative capacity.
Examine the significance of judicial intervention in upholding electoral integrity at the grassroots level and its implications for democratic decentralization.
Suggest reforms to strengthen the autonomy and functioning of State Election Commissions and to prevent irregularities in delimitation exercises.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with a blurred background of a court building, symbolizing electoral justice.
articles.trivia: The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendment Acts gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions and Urban Local Bodies respectively.

articles.headline: Conservation Efforts for Great Indian Bustard Show Promising Results in Rajasthan and Gujarat
articles.explainer:
• Recent surveys indicate a slight but significant increase in the population of the critically endangered Great Indian Bustard (GIB) in their key habitats in Rajasthan and Gujarat.
• This positive trend is attributed to sustained conservation efforts under Project Great Indian Bustard, including habitat improvement, captive breeding programs, and power line mitigation measures.
• The 'Bustard Breeding Centre' in Jaisalmer has successfully hatched several chicks, contributing to the species' recovery.
• Challenges remain, primarily habitat fragmentation, poaching, and collision with power lines and wind turbines, necessitating continued vigilance and policy interventions.
• The success highlights the importance of collaborative efforts between forest departments, local communities, and scientific institutions in protecting critically endangered species.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: The Great Indian Bustard (GIB) is one of the heaviest flying birds in the world.
It is listed as 'Critically Endangered' on the IUCN Red List.
It is primarily found in Rajasthan and Gujarat, with smaller populations in Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Andhra Pradesh.
It is listed in Schedule I of the Indian Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972.
Project Great Indian Bustard was launched by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted challenges faced in conserving critically endangered species like the Great Indian Bustard in India and the strategies employed to address them.
Analyze the effectiveness of Project Great Indian Bustard's approach, including captive breeding and power line mitigation, in increasing the GIB population.
Examine the importance of community participation and inter-state cooperation in successful wildlife conservation efforts in India.
Evaluate the ecological significance of the GIB as a flagship species of grassland ecosystems and the broader implications of its conservation for biodiversity.
articles.imageDescription: A Great Indian Bustard walking in its natural grassland habitat.
articles.trivia: The GIB is the state bird of Rajasthan.

highlights: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutionality of Uniform Civil Code Implementation, paving the way for phased reforms.
RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% for sixth consecutive time, balancing growth and inflation amidst global pressures.
ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle 'Pushpak-2', aiming for cheaper space access.
Government Launches 'National Urban Air Quality Improvement Mission' to tackle city pollution in 150 non-attainment cities.
India Pledges Strong Support for African Union's Permanent Seat in UNSC, strengthening South-South cooperation.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India, consider the following statements:
1. Article 44 of the Indian Constitution mandates the State to secure a UCC for its citizens.
2. Goa is the only Indian state currently having a Uniform Civil Code.
3. The Supreme Court has, in its latest ruling, directed the immediate nationwide implementation of the UCC.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Article 44 is a Directive Principle of State Policy. Statement 2 is correct. Goa has a common family law, the Goa Civil Code, which is a UCC. Statement 3 is incorrect. The Supreme Court upheld the constitutional validity of the framework for implementing UCC but did not direct immediate nationwide implementation. The implementation process remains with the legislative and executive.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India. The RBI Governor is the ex-officio Chairperson of the MPC, but the decision is taken by the committee.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program:
1. The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) project aims to develop a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) launch vehicle.
2. 'Pushpak-2' is the latest prototype successfully tested by ISRO for controlled landing experiments.
3. The primary objective of reusable launch vehicles is to reduce the cost of space access.
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The RLV-TD project is ISRO's long-term goal for a TSTO RLV. 'Pushpak-2' represents a significant step in this direction with successful testing. The core motivation for reusable launch vehicles globally, including for ISRO, is to significantly reduce the cost of space launches.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following are correctly matched regarding India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)?
1. Aadhaar: Unique identification system.
2. UPI: Real-time digital payment system.
3. DigiLocker: Platform for issuing and verifying digital documents.
4. ONDC: Open Network for Digital Commerce.
prelimsMocks.explanation: All the given pairs are correctly matched. Aadhaar is a unique identity, UPI facilitates digital payments, DigiLocker stores digital documents, and ONDC is a recent initiative for digital commerce.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Great Indian Bustard (GIB), consider the following statements:
1. It is listed as 'Critically Endangered' in the IUCN Red List.
2. It is the state bird of Rajasthan.
3. Project Great Indian Bustard primarily focuses on in-situ conservation with no captive breeding component.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. GIB is critically endangered and the state bird of Rajasthan. Statement 3 is incorrect. Project Great Indian Bustard includes both in-situ conservation and significant captive breeding components, such as the Bustard Breeding Centre in Jaisalmer.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's verdict on the Uniform Civil Code marks a pivotal moment in India's legal and social landscape. Discuss the constitutional implications of this judgment and the challenges inherent in harmonizing personal laws across a diverse nation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the UCC and Article 44. Explain the SC's ruling on constitutional validity. Discuss implications for gender justice, secularism, and national integration. Detail the challenges: diversity of personal laws, potential for social unrest, federal concerns, and implementation complexities. Conclude with the need for a consultative and phased approach.

mainsMocks.question: India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model has garnered global attention for its potential to drive inclusive growth and efficient governance. Analyze the key features of India's DPI stack and evaluate its role in fostering financial inclusion and digital transformation in the country. To what extent can this model be replicated by other developing nations?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
mainsMocks.approach: Define DPI and introduce the India Stack (Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker, ONDC). Explain how these components foster financial inclusion (e.g., JAM trinity, digital payments) and governance (e.g., paperless, cashless, faceless services). Discuss success metrics. Analyze replicability for other nations, considering factors like digital literacy, regulatory environment, data privacy, and infrastructural readiness. Conclude with India's role in promoting DPI as a global public good.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant efforts, urban air pollution remains a critical environmental and public health challenge in India. Critically examine the 'National Urban Air Quality Improvement Mission' (NUQIM) in addressing this issue. What are the persistent challenges and what more needs to be done to achieve sustainable air quality across Indian cities?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Disaster and disaster management.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the severity of urban air pollution in India. Explain NUQIM's objectives, multi-pronged strategy (emission norms, EVs, public transport, green planning), and performance-linked funding. Critically analyze its strengths over previous programs (e.g., NCAP) and persistent challenges (e.g., implementation gaps, funding, inter-agency coordination, behavioral change, transboundary pollution). Suggest further measures like robust enforcement, citizen participation, and regional cooperation for sustainable outcomes.

quickBytes.text: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution comes under the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP).
The Repo Rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks in India.
The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) constructs and maintains roads in India's border areas.
ISRO's 'Pushpak-2' is a prototype for a Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV), aiming to reduce space launch costs.
The G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting discusses global economic and financial issues.
The Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution deals with the Anti-Defection Law.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
The African Union (AU) is a continental union of 55 member states located on the continent of Africa.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
The Great Indian Bustard (GIB) is listed as 'Critically Endangered' by IUCN.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutionality of Uniform Civil Code Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• A nine-judge bench of the Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, upheld the constitutional validity of the Union Government's framework for implementing a Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
• The Court affirmed Parliament's legislative competence to enact a UCC, stating it aligns with Article 44 of the Directive Principles of State Policy.
• While acknowledging concerns about diversity, the judgment emphasized that a UCC aims for gender justice and equality, harmonizing personal laws without eroding essential religious practices.
• The ruling provides a significant legal impetus for the government to proceed with a phased implementation strategy, potentially starting with specific aspects of personal law.
• Dissenting opinions focused on the potential impact on minority rights and the need for broader societal consensus before such a sweeping reform.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Article 44 mandates the state to endeavor to secure a UCC for citizens.
UCC is listed under Part IV of the Indian Constitution (DPSP).
The Supreme Court has previously called for the implementation of UCC in several cases.
The judgment was delivered by a nine-judge bench, indicating a matter of significant constitutional importance.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on the future of personal laws in India and its alignment with the principles of gender justice and equality.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in implementing a Uniform Civil Code, considering India's diverse socio-religious fabric and the federal structure.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in interpreting constitutional provisions like Article 44 and shaping social reforms in the country.
Examine the arguments for and against the UCC, including concerns about minority rights versus the pursuit of national integration.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of the Supreme Court of India building with legal documents and scales of justice.
GS2.trivia: Goa is currently the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code, inherited from its Portuguese colonial past.

GS2.headline: Manipur MLA Vungzagin Valte Passes Away at 61
GS2.explainer:
• Vungzagin Valte, a sitting Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Manipur, passed away at the age of 61.
• He represented the Thanlon Assembly constituency in Pherzawl district, having been elected multiple times since 2012.
• Valte was a prominent political figure in the Kuki-Zo community and had served in various capacities, including as a cabinet minister.
• His demise creates a vacancy in the Manipur Legislative Assembly, necessitating a by-election within six months as per constitutional provisions.
• The political landscape of Manipur, particularly in the hill districts, is likely to see shifts following the loss of a seasoned leader.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: An MLA is a representative elected by the voters of an electoral district (constituency) to the legislature of a state government in the Indian system.
A vacancy in the state legislative assembly requires a by-election within six months.
Manipur is a state in Northeast India.
The strength of a state legislative assembly varies based on population and is determined by constitutional provisions (Article 170).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of regional political leadership in maintaining stability and representing diverse communities in states like Manipur.
Analyze the constitutional provisions and electoral process related to filling vacancies in state legislative assemblies through by-elections.
Examine the challenges faced by electoral democracy in India's North-Eastern states, including issues of representation and political participation.
GS2.imageDescription: Portrait photo of Vungzagin Valte, former Manipur MLA.
GS2.trivia: The minimum age to be an MLA in India is 25 years.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Focus on Indo-Pacific Security and Green Economy
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming their commitment to a Free and Open Indo-Pacific (FOIP).
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, maritime security, and intelligence sharing, particularly concerning regional challenges.
• Both nations pledged to deepen collaboration in emerging technologies, including quantum computing, AI, and semiconductors, to build resilient supply chains.
• A significant outcome was the agreement to fast-track joint projects in green energy, hydrogen production, and electric vehicle infrastructure.
• The dialogue also emphasized strengthening multilateral cooperation through platforms like the Quad and the G20, addressing global issues such as climate change and sustainable development.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) alongside the USA and Australia.
Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
Both countries participate in joint military exercises such as Dharma Guardian (Army) and JIMEX (Navy).
The concept of a 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' is a cornerstone of their shared vision.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving strategic convergence between India and Japan in the Indo-Pacific region, citing key areas of cooperation and shared interests.
Discuss the significance of the India-Japan partnership in promoting regional stability and economic prosperity, particularly in the context of geopolitical shifts.
Evaluate the potential for enhanced collaboration in critical and emerging technologies between India and Japan, and its implications for national security and economic resilience.
Examine how the bilateral relationship contributes to India's 'Act East Policy' and Japan's broader foreign policy objectives.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags displayed during a joint press conference.
GS2.trivia: Japan is India's largest ODA (Official Development Assistance) partner.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Health Unveils New National Mental Health Action Plan 2026-2030
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched the 'National Mental Health Action Plan 2026-2030', a five-year roadmap to strengthen mental healthcare services across India.
• The plan focuses on integrating mental health into primary healthcare, increasing the availability of trained professionals, and leveraging digital health solutions.
• Key objectives include universal screening for common mental disorders, reducing the treatment gap, and combating stigma associated with mental illness.
• It allocates increased funding for mental health infrastructure, including district mental health programmes and tele-manas services.
• The action plan emphasizes a community-based approach, promoting mental well-being and early intervention, with a special focus on vulnerable populations like adolescents and the elderly.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: The Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, decriminalized suicide and provides for rights of persons with mental illness.
National Mental Health Programme (NMHP) was launched in 1982.
Tele-MANAS (Tele-Mental Health Assistance and Networking Across States) is a 24/7 mental health helpline.
India faces a significant mental health treatment gap.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the 'National Mental Health Action Plan 2026-2030', highlighting its strengths in addressing India's mental health crisis and potential challenges in implementation.
Discuss the importance of integrating mental healthcare into primary health services and the role of digital platforms like Tele-MANAS in expanding access.
Examine the socio-economic factors contributing to mental health issues in India and the societal challenges in combating stigma and promoting mental well-being.
Suggest further policy measures and community-level interventions that could enhance the effectiveness of mental health initiatives in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Symbolic image of a brain with intertwined hands, representing mental health support and care.
GS2.trivia: Globally, one in eight people live with a mental disorder.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms in Anti-Defection Law
GS2.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee submitted its report recommending significant amendments to the Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution, commonly known as the Anti-Defection Law.
• The committee proposed restricting the Speaker's discretionary powers in deciding defection cases and suggested a fixed timeframe for such decisions, possibly within three months.
• It also recommended clarifying the definition of 'voluntary giving up of membership' and 'merger' to prevent misuse and ensure legislative stability.
• The report advocated for the Election Commission's role in advising on defection cases to ensure impartiality and reduce political influence.
• These reforms aim to curb frequent political defections, strengthen party discipline, and uphold the spirit of democratic mandates.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: The Tenth Schedule was added to the Indian Constitution by the 52nd Amendment Act of 1985.
The law applies to both Parliament and State Legislatures.
The Speaker (or Chairman in Rajya Sabha) is the final authority in deciding defection cases.
The Supreme Court in Kihoto Hollohan v. Zachillhu (1992) upheld the constitutional validity of the Tenth Schedule but subjected the Speaker's decision to judicial review.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the effectiveness of the Anti-Defection Law in curbing political defections and maintaining legislative stability in India since its enactment.
Discuss the challenges arising from the Speaker's discretionary powers in deciding defection cases and how the proposed reforms aim to address these issues.
Analyze the implications of involving the Election Commission in advising on defection cases for ensuring impartiality and strengthening democratic processes.
Suggest other potential reforms or constitutional amendments that could further enhance the efficacy of the Anti-Defection Law and prevent its misuse.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building with a gavel and legal documents, symbolizing legislative reforms.
GS2.trivia: The 91st Amendment Act of 2003 removed the provision that allowed a split in a political party (one-third of members) to be exempt from defection.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Strong Support for African Union's Permanent Seat in UNSC
GS2.explainer:
• During a high-level visit to the African Union (AU) headquarters, India's External Affairs Minister reiterated India's strong and unequivocal support for a permanent seat for the African Union in a reformed United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
• This pledge aligns with India's consistent call for comprehensive UNSC reforms to make it more representative, legitimate, and effective.
• India emphasized the need for greater representation of developing countries, particularly from Africa, which has historically been underrepresented in global decision-making bodies.
• The move is seen as a significant diplomatic gesture to strengthen India-Africa relations and foster South-South cooperation.
• Both sides stressed the importance of multilateralism and collective efforts to address global challenges, including climate change, terrorism, and sustainable development.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The UNSC has 15 members: 5 permanent (P5) and 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.
The P5 members are China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States.
India is a member of the G4 group (along with Brazil, Germany, and Japan) that advocates for permanent seats for themselves and Africa in the UNSC.
The African Union consists of 55 member states located on the continent of Africa.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind India's consistent support for the African Union's permanent seat in a reformed UNSC and its implications for India's foreign policy objectives.
Discuss the arguments for and against the expansion of the UNSC's permanent membership, considering issues of effectiveness, representation, and geopolitical balance.
Examine the broader significance of India-Africa cooperation in shaping a more equitable and representative global order, particularly through multilateral institutions.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving comprehensive UN Security Council reforms and the role of various stakeholders in this process.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the African Union displayed side-by-side during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: The African Union was formed in 2002, succeeding the Organisation of African Unity (OAU).

GS2.headline: Report Highlights Challenges in Implementing National Education Policy 2020
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by the Ministry of Education highlighted significant challenges in the nationwide implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Key issues identified include inadequate funding for infrastructure upgrades, shortage of trained teachers, especially for foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) programs, and resistance to curriculum reforms.
• The report also pointed to disparities in implementation across states, with some lagging due to administrative hurdles and lack of decentralized planning.
• While acknowledging progress in certain areas like digital learning initiatives, the report called for greater inter-ministerial coordination and community involvement.
• Recommendations include increased budgetary allocation, focused teacher training programs, and flexible implementation strategies tailored to regional contexts.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
Its vision is to transform India's education system to meet the needs of the 21st century.
Key pillars include access, equity, quality, affordability, and accountability.
The policy proposes a new 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the major challenges identified in the implementation of the National Education Policy 2020 across different levels of education in India.
Discuss the importance of adequate funding, teacher capacity building, and decentralized planning for the successful realization of NEP's objectives.
Examine how the disparities in implementation across states can exacerbate educational inequalities and suggest strategies to foster equitable progress.
Evaluate the long-term implications of successful or unsuccessful NEP implementation on India's human resource development and economic growth.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a classroom setting with a teacher, symbolizing education and learning.
GS2.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) recommended spending 6% of GDP on education, a target yet to be consistently met.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education

GS2.headline: High Court Stays Local Body Elections in State X Due to Delimitation Irregularities
GS2.explainer:
• The High Court of State X issued an interim stay on the upcoming local body elections, citing significant irregularities in the delimitation process of wards and constituencies.
• Petitions filed by various political parties and civil society groups alleged that the delimitation exercise was conducted arbitrarily, violating principles of population equality and geographical compactness.
• The court observed prima facie evidence of gerrymandering and ordered the State Election Commission (SEC) to review and rectify the delimitation process within a specified timeframe.
• This decision has led to political uproar and logistical challenges for the SEC, which had already initiated election preparations.
• The ruling underscores the judiciary's role in upholding electoral fairness and ensuring the constitutional mandate of free and fair elections at the grassroots level.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Local body elections (Panchayats and Municipalities) are conducted by the State Election Commission (SEC).
Delimitation is the act or process of fixing limits or boundaries of territorial constituencies in a country or a province having a legislative body.
Articles 243K and 243ZA of the Constitution deal with elections to Panchayats and Municipalities, respectively.
The High Court has powers of judicial review over the decisions of the SEC, especially concerning delimitation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the importance of fair and transparent delimitation processes for ensuring equitable representation in local self-governance institutions in India.
Discuss the constitutional role of the State Election Commission (SEC) in conducting local body elections and the challenges it faces, including political interference and administrative capacity.
Examine the significance of judicial intervention in upholding electoral integrity at the grassroots level and its implications for democratic decentralization.
Suggest reforms to strengthen the autonomy and functioning of State Election Commissions and to prevent irregularities in delimitation exercises.
GS2.imageDescription: A ballot box with a blurred background of a court building, symbolizing electoral justice.
GS2.trivia: The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendment Acts gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions and Urban Local Bodies respectively.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Global Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive policy review.
• This decision comes amidst persistent global inflationary pressures and geopolitical uncertainties, balancing growth objectives with inflation control.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted concerns over food inflation and the need to ensure inflation aligns with the 4% target on a durable basis.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation remains within the target while supporting growth.
• Market analysts anticipate a 'higher for longer' interest rate regime, with potential rate cuts pushed further into the year.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: The Repo Rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks in the event of any shortfall of funds.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
RBI's inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst current domestic and global economic conditions.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth, particularly in the context of supply-side pressures and global spillovers.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing persistent inflation and supporting sustainable economic recovery in India.
Examine the implications of a 'higher for longer' interest rate regime on various sectors of the Indian economy, including investment, consumption, and credit growth.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade with monetary policy charts in the background.
GS3.trivia: The RBI was established in 1935 under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype 'Pushpak-2'
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful experimental flight test of its advanced Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype, codenamed 'Pushpak-2'.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment simulating a re-entry trajectory, demonstrating autonomous navigation, guidance, and control systems.
• This marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) launch vehicle, aiming to drastically reduce space launch costs.
• The 'Pushpak-2' incorporates advanced heat shield materials and aerodynamic designs optimized for atmospheric re-entry and hypersonic flight.
• ISRO aims to achieve operational reusable launch capabilities within the next decade, bolstering India's competitive edge in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) project began with the winged body 'HEX' mission.
Reusable launch vehicles aim to reduce the cost of space access by avoiding the need to build new rockets for each launch.
Key challenges in RLV development include heat management during re-entry and precise landing mechanisms.
Global players like SpaceX (Falcon 9) and Blue Origin are also pursuing reusable rocket technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its potential impact on commercial space exploration.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in designing and testing RLVs, focusing on aspects like materials science, aerodynamics, and propulsion systems.
Evaluate how 'Pushpak-2' and future RLV advancements can enhance India's self-reliance in space and contribute to its economic development.
Compare India's RLV efforts with global trends in reusable space technology and assess its competitive position.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's 'Pushpak-2' RLV prototype during atmospheric re-entry.
GS3.trivia: The name 'Pushpak' is derived from the mythical flying chariot Pushpaka Vimana from the ancient Indian epic Ramayana.

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'National Urban Air Quality Improvement Mission' to Tackle City Pollution
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched the 'National Urban Air Quality Improvement Mission' (NUQIM), a comprehensive initiative targeting air pollution in 150 non-attainment cities.
• The mission adopts a multi-pronged strategy, including stricter emission norms for industries, promotion of electric vehicles, enhanced public transport, and green urban planning.
• It introduces a performance-linked funding mechanism for states and urban local bodies based on their progress in reducing PM2.5 and PM10 levels.
• NUQIM also emphasizes public awareness campaigns, capacity building for local authorities, and the deployment of advanced air quality monitoring networks.
• This initiative is an extension of previous efforts like the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) but with a stronger focus on integrated urban planning and citizen participation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
keyTerms: National Urban Air Quality Improvement Mission (NUQIM)
prelimsPoints: PM2.5 and PM10 are fine particulate matter linked to respiratory and cardiovascular diseases.
Non-attainment cities are those that consistently fail to meet national ambient air quality standards.
The National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) was launched in 2019 to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024 (from 2017 levels).
Air Quality Index (AQI) is a tool used to communicate air quality status to people.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the 'National Urban Air Quality Improvement Mission' (NUQIM) in comparison to previous initiatives like NCAP, highlighting its strengths and potential weaknesses.
Discuss the multi-sectoral approach required to tackle urban air pollution in India, considering its various sources and socio-economic dimensions.
Examine the role of technology, policy, and public participation in achieving sustainable air quality improvements in Indian cities.
Analyze the challenges in implementing such missions across diverse urban landscapes and the importance of inter-state and inter-agency coordination.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic showing a clean city skyline with green trees and minimal smog, representing improved air quality.
GS3.trivia: According to WHO, air pollution is the largest environmental health risk globally.

GS3.headline: Border Roads Organisation Inaugurates Strategic Tunnel in Arunachal Pradesh, Boosting Border Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) inaugurated the 'Sela-Tawang Tunnel Bypass', a crucial strategic tunnel connecting Bomdila with Tawang in Arunachal Pradesh.
• The 2.5 km twin-tube tunnel, along with an approach road, provides all-weather connectivity, significantly reducing travel time and improving military logistics to the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• Constructed at an altitude of over 13,000 feet, the tunnel bypasses the challenging Sela Pass, which often remains snow-bound for several months a year.
• This infrastructure project is part of India's broader strategy to enhance connectivity and develop border infrastructure in the Eastern Himalayas.
• The tunnel will not only aid defence forces but also boost tourism and socio-economic development in the remote Tawang region.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is a road construction executive force in India that develops and maintains road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.
Sela Pass is a high-altitude mountain pass in Arunachal Pradesh.
Tawang is a strategically important town in Arunachal Pradesh, close to the LAC.
