The tunnel project is part of the larger Sela Tunnel project which includes two tunnels and an approach road.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of border infrastructure development, such as the Sela-Tawang Tunnel Bypass, for India's national security and military preparedness along the LAC.
Analyze the role of the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) in enhancing connectivity in challenging terrains and its contribution to India's defence capabilities.
Examine the socio-economic impact of improved connectivity in remote border regions like Tawang, including its potential for tourism and local development.
Evaluate the challenges involved in constructing high-altitude infrastructure projects and the innovative engineering solutions employed by agencies like BRO.
GS3.imageDescription: Entrance of the newly inaugurated Sela-Tawang Tunnel in a mountainous region.
GS3.trivia: BRO was formed on 7 May 1960 and operates under the Ministry of Defence.

GS3.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Gains Traction at G20 Finance Ministers Meet
GS3.explainer:
• India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, exemplified by Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, received significant praise and interest at the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting.
• Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman presented the success story of India's DPI in driving financial inclusion, digital payments, and efficient public service delivery.
• Several G20 nations and international organizations expressed keen interest in adopting or adapting India's open-source, interoperable DPI frameworks for their own digital transformation.
• Discussions focused on cross-border payments, data governance, and building inclusive digital economies using lessons from the Indian model.
• India reaffirmed its commitment to sharing its DPI expertise with developing nations, promoting a 'global good' approach to digital transformation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number issued by the UIDAI.
UPI is an instant real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
DigiLocker is a cloud-based platform for issuance and verification of documents.
India's DPI stack is often referred to as the 'India Stack'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model in fostering financial inclusion, governance, and economic growth within India and for other developing nations.
Discuss the key components of the India Stack (e.g., Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker) and how their interoperability drives efficiency and innovation in public service delivery.
Evaluate the opportunities and challenges for other G20 nations in adopting or adapting India's DPI model, considering issues of data privacy, regulatory frameworks, and technological scalability.
Examine India's role in promoting a global digital public good and its implications for international cooperation in digital transformation.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating interconnected digital services like UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker, representing India's DPI.
GS3.trivia: India's UPI system processes more transactions than credit cards and debit cards combined in India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Inclusive growth and issues arising from it

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework for Green Hydrogen Production and Export
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government launched a comprehensive 'National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0' policy framework, aimed at accelerating domestic production and positioning India as a global hub for green hydrogen.
• The policy introduces enhanced incentives for green hydrogen and green ammonia production, including capital subsidies and production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolyzer manufacturing.
• It mandates blending of green hydrogen in specific industrial sectors (e.g., refineries, fertilizer production) and promotes its use in transport and energy storage.
• The framework also outlines strategies for developing a robust green hydrogen export ecosystem, including infrastructure for storage and transportation.
• This initiative is critical for India's energy transition, decarbonization goals, and achieving net-zero emissions by 2070.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India launched its initial National Green Hydrogen Mission in January 2023.
Green ammonia is produced using green hydrogen and nitrogen, with renewable energy sources.
Electrolyzers are devices that split water into hydrogen and oxygen.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the 'National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0' policy framework in accelerating India's energy transition and achieving its climate targets.
Discuss the economic and technological challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production in India and the role of government incentives in overcoming them.
Evaluate India's potential to become a global hub for green hydrogen export, considering its renewable energy resources and strategic location.
Examine the cross-sectoral applications of green hydrogen and its role in decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and transportation.
GS3.imageDescription: Stylized image showing green hydrogen flame and renewable energy sources like wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the Universe.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous 6G Technology Development, India Aims for Global Leadership
GS3.explainer:
• Indian researchers at IIT-Delhi and the Department of Telecommunications (DoT) announced a significant breakthrough in the development of indigenous 6G communication technology.
• The achievement involves successful lab-scale demonstration of terahertz communication modules and AI-driven network management protocols, crucial for future 6G networks.
• India's 'Bharat 6G Alliance' (B6GA) is accelerating research and development, aiming to establish India as a key contributor to global 6G standards.
• The government plans to allocate substantial funding for R&D, pilot projects, and international collaborations to fast-track commercial deployment by 2030.
• This initiative is poised to enhance India's digital sovereignty, create new economic opportunities, and bridge the digital divide in remote areas.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: 6G is the successor to 5G technology, expected to offer significantly higher speeds, lower latency, and greater capacity.
Terahertz frequencies (0.1 THz to 10 THz) are being explored for 6G for ultra-high data rates.
AI and Machine Learning are expected to play a crucial role in managing and optimizing 6G networks.
India launched the Bharat 6G Alliance (B6GA) in 2023 to foster innovation in 6G technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential transformative impact of indigenous 6G technology on various sectors of the Indian economy and society, including healthcare, education, and manufacturing.
Analyze the technological advancements and challenges associated with 6G development, particularly focusing on terahertz communication and AI integration.
Evaluate the strategic importance of India's efforts to achieve global leadership in 6G, considering aspects of digital sovereignty, economic competitiveness, and national security.
Examine the role of government, academia, and industry collaboration through initiatives like B6GA in driving technological innovation and policy formulation for future communication networks.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract digital graphic representing 6G network connectivity with speed lines and data flow.
GS3.trivia: The first generation (1G) of mobile telephony was introduced in the 1980s, offering analog voice calls.

GS3.headline: SEBI Proposes Stricter Norms for Social Stock Exchange Listings
GS3.explainer:
• The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) released a consultation paper proposing stricter eligibility and disclosure norms for entities listing on the Social Stock Exchange (SSE).
• The proposed amendments aim to enhance transparency, accountability, and investor confidence in the SSE, ensuring that only genuine social enterprises benefit from public funding.
• Key proposals include mandating impact reporting by independent auditors, increasing minimum issue size for fundraising, and stringent criteria for defining 'social intent and impact'.
• The move comes amidst concerns about potential 'impact washing' and the need to protect investors in this nascent segment of the capital market.
• Stakeholders are invited to provide feedback on the proposed regulations, which are expected to streamline operations and strengthen the SSE ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Government Budgeting.
keyTerms: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
prelimsPoints: The Social Stock Exchange (SSE) was introduced in India in 2022 to facilitate fundraising for social enterprises.
SEBI is the regulatory body for the securities market in India.
Social enterprises can raise funds through equity, debt, or Zero Coupon Zero Principal (ZCZP) instruments on the SSE.
The concept of SSE was recommended by a working group set up by SEBI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives behind the establishment of the Social Stock Exchange (SSE) in India and its potential to channel capital towards social development.
Discuss the rationale behind SEBI's proposed stricter norms for SSE listings, considering the challenges of 'impact washing' and ensuring investor protection.
Evaluate the role of impact reporting and independent audits in fostering transparency and accountability within the social enterprise ecosystem.
Examine how a well-regulated SSE can contribute to inclusive growth and address societal challenges by providing a credible fundraising platform for non-profit and for-profit social enterprises.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic depicting a stock market ticker with 'SSE' and 'Impact' keywords, symbolizing the Social Stock Exchange.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the few countries globally to have a dedicated Social Stock Exchange.

GS3.headline: Conservation Efforts for Great Indian Bustard Show Promising Results in Rajasthan and Gujarat
GS3.explainer:
• Recent surveys indicate a slight but significant increase in the population of the critically endangered Great Indian Bustard (GIB) in their key habitats in Rajasthan and Gujarat.
• This positive trend is attributed to sustained conservation efforts under Project Great Indian Bustard, including habitat improvement, captive breeding programs, and power line mitigation measures.
• The 'Bustard Breeding Centre' in Jaisalmer has successfully hatched several chicks, contributing to the species' recovery.
• Challenges remain, primarily habitat fragmentation, poaching, and collision with power lines and wind turbines, necessitating continued vigilance and policy interventions.
• The success highlights the importance of collaborative efforts between forest departments, local communities, and scientific institutions in protecting critically endangered species.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: The Great Indian Bustard (GIB) is one of the heaviest flying birds in the world.
It is listed as 'Critically Endangered' on the IUCN Red List.
It is primarily found in Rajasthan and Gujarat, with smaller populations in Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Andhra Pradesh.
It is listed in Schedule I of the Indian Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972.
Project Great Indian Bustard was launched by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted challenges faced in conserving critically endangered species like the Great Indian Bustard in India and the strategies employed to address them.
Analyze the effectiveness of Project Great Indian Bustard's approach, including captive breeding and power line mitigation, in increasing the GIB population.
Examine the importance of community participation and inter-state cooperation in successful wildlife conservation efforts in India.
Evaluate the ecological significance of the GIB as a flagship species of grassland ecosystems and the broader implications of its conservation for biodiversity.
GS3.imageDescription: A Great Indian Bustard walking in its natural grassland habitat.
GS3.trivia: The GIB is the state bird of Rajasthan.
